helpful: statpack per-Term and relist/CVSG cut tables — the main quantitative anchor
provisioned petition.txt and questions-presented.txt — full merits context without any fetching
snapshot's ProceedingsandOrder — distribution date and missing-BIO signal
gaps: CourtListener MCP arrived rate-exhausted (HTTP 429, 1200/day shared quota) — a per-cell quota reservation or a docket-delta feed vs. the snapshot would make the live check reliable

fedcourts query has no issue/topic facet on SCOTUS rows, so it cannot surface doctrinally similar cert priors (e.g. Fourth Amendment petitions); a QP-text similarity search over resolved petitions would be the single most useful addition
statpack.md no longer carries the per-salience-band table the prompt references; the per-fee-class (paid vs IFP) grant rates also only ride in statpack.json, which is not committed alongside
notes: The broad-filter corpus query cost 431 ranged GETs / ~113 MB for 8 rows; without a topic facet on SCOTUS rows its marginal value per byte is low for cert cells.
